steps.title: Identify the clause structure around the blank
steps.content: The first side of the blank is the noun phrase US civil rights history, and the second side begins with the participial phrase the former establishing.... Participial phrases modifying a noun typically require a comma to separate them, not a period or semicolon.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for noun + participial phrase
steps.content:
Choice B adds ‘, the’ to create US civil rights history, the former establishing..., which correctly uses a comma to separate the noun from the explanatory participial phrase.

Choices A, C, and
  • D use or omit punctuation that disrupts this relationship (A creates a run-on
  • C adds an unnecessary subordinating conjunction
  • D splits the sentence incorrectly into two fragments)
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
